Customer Trends & Competitor Intel: The shop has experienced an extreme amount of declines over the past few months. Gabe primarily uses American First, but noted their approval rates aren't much better right now.

Coaching Provided: Reviewed common decline reasons with Gabe, including typos, bad credit, poor bank account management, and the use of meta banks. Highlighted that we are currently seeing a large number of credit freezes and typos system-wide as potential factors.

Subject: 00UTO00000YPuMn2AL
_label_Company / Account: J6 Properties, LLC DBA Christian Brothers Spring Stuebner
Full Comments:
Checked in via Maps Application 
Met with Robert to review March performance, discuss their active pipeline, and set incentive goals for the new month. We remain their first choice for financing, and overall shop traffic continues to be steady.

Meeting Notes:

Performance Review: Discussed March numbers; the shop came in just under $3k short of their goal, but the high number of submitted applications demonstrated strong effort.

Active Pipeline: Robert currently has 2 customers with pending jobs that he is waiting to finish and finalize.

Incentives: Offered to work on securing another shop lunch (similar to what we did in February) if they can get off to a fast start this month.

Competitor Intel & Positioning: We are still their first choice, though we occasionally lose some business to Affirm when customers automatically opt into it.

Business Trends: Car count at the shop has been very steady.

Next Steps: Made Robert aware that Noemy will be reaching out to him soon.
_label_Full Comments:

• No financing providers currently in use
• Decision-maker (Perla) not present during visit
• Small operation (1–2 bays) with limited staff
• No marketing/signage in place
• Opportunity to introduce EasyPay

Subject: 00UTO00000YPxRA2A1
Full Comments:
Checked in via Maps Application 
Spoke with Anthony and Kevin, the new managers at the location, to review last month's performance and set goals for this month. We remain the first choice for financing here, and I am ordering new marketing materials to support their volume.

Meeting Notes:

Point of Contact: Met with Anthony and Kevin, who recently took over as the new managers at this location.

Performance Review: The shop missed the $12k volume mark for last month by just under $3k.

Incentives & Goals: Promised a team lunch if they can get off to a fast start this month to make up for the recent shortfall. Kevin specifically requested pizza for their next lunch reward.

Account Positioning: Confirmed that we are still the first choice for financing at this location.

Action Items: Currently ordering new marketing materials. I need to make sure I drop off QR codes during my next pass through the area.

This merchant has been an EasyPay customer since 2016, showing a long-standing relationship of nearly 10 years. The store appears well-established, with a large inventory and its own delivery truck, suggesting strong operational capacity and sales potential.

The location is situated in a high-traffic area, presenting significant opportunity for financing growth once engagement is re-established.

Key Insights:
• Store closed during visit
• Long-term EasyPay customer (since 2016)
• Competitor marketing present (Koalafi, Snap)
• Established business with delivery capabilities
• High-traffic location with strong growth potential
